page: Amazon Web Services c7a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs using the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor operating at 3.7 GHz. The memory configuration consists of 96.0 GB of DDR5 RAM running at 5 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c7a.12xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance, particularly in multi-core CPU execution and software compilation tasks, placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read bandwidth for small cached blocks and strong performance for large uncached blocks. The processor's support for advanced vector and matrix extensions, including AVX-512 and AVX-512_VNNI, enables strong performance in cryptographic operations, image processing, and medium-scale LLM inference prompt processing.

The instance represents a highly resource-dense compute profile, trading off local storage and GPU acceleration for high-frequency dedicated CPU cores and fast DDR5 memory. This makes it highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. It is well-suited for workloads such as software compilation, high-throughput web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 96.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance and software compilation speeds, alongside strong memory bandwidth and cryptographic capabilities. This hardware profile is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, database operations, high-throughput static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 19 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-performance multi-core workloads.

tagline: A dedicated 48-core compute-optimized platform featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and top-tier multi-core processing performance for demanding enterprise workloads.

96.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and software compilation performance
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for CPU-bound tasks, database operations, and web serving
